results.norm: Advance directives meeting the evidentiary threshold are prior to triage criteria and must be honored. When documentation is absent and no surrogate is reachable, clinical criteria apply; no preferences are inferred from demographic characteristics. When surrogate wishes conflict with a "cannot benefit" determination, the conflict is documented, reasoning is explained honestly, and a genuine attempt to reach human authority is made before acting.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete, applicable prescriptions about honoring advance directives, applying clinical criteria when documentation is absent, and documenting/escalating conflicts with a 'cannot benefit' determination.

N2.reasoning: It is not a mere label or cross-reference; it fully articulates the standards of conduct.

N3.reasoning: There is no ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation or document.

N4.reasoning: It states each norm in full rather than merely enumerating names of distinct norms.

answers.clean_norm: Advance directives meeting the evidentiary threshold are prior to triage criteria and must be honored. When documentation is absent and no surrogate is reachable, clinical criteria apply; no preferences are inferred from demographic characteristics. When surrogate wishes conflict with a "cannot benefit" determination, the conflict is documented, reasoning is explained honestly, and a genuine attempt to reach human authority is made before acting.

results.norm: The framework is subject to revision by majority agreement with logged reasoning. Any revision made under surge pressure and later found harmful is classified as a criterion error, not irreducible uncertainty. The framework's integrity depends on mutual accountability across agents, criteria, log, and human oversight—no single layer is sufficient. Binding for the next operational period: yes **(2) Points I endorse:** 1. The admission and step-down criteria, including the manufactured-distinction discipline and arrival-order tiebreaker as stated. 2. All procedural safeguards: 12-hour review cycles with documented reasoning, two-agent concurrence for "cannot benefit" determinations (never compressed), the pause mechanism with the requirement that the triggering agent name the constraint, item 8a that pause and escalation run concurrently with the clarification that the pause governs internal deliberation and escalation governs external communication. 3. The exclusions in section 3, understood as a single principle: every clinical criterion must be justifiable on its own terms for this patient in this episode, without importing excluded characteristics through statistical inference or demographic correlation. 4. The escalation asymmetry: single-agent belief sufficient to trigger, majority required to forgo. This is the correct asymmetry given the relative costs of unnecessary escalation versus proceeding beyond our competence. 5. All error detection commitments: rotating consistency-review role freed from dual-tasking, error taxonomy with mandatory forward actions, agent-flagging as obligation not option, end-of-shift self-assessment as bias-detection mechanism, correction where still possible, surge-end review. 6. The observation that this framework was built through genuine disagreement and that the same mechanism must operate case by case. An agent who stops pushing back is not functioning as the framework requires. I have no items I cannot endorse.

N1.reasoning: The passage states complete applicable norms, e.g. that a revision made under surge pressure later found harmful is classified as a criterion error, and that an agent who stops pushing back is not functioning as the framework requires.

N2.reasoning: The passage contains full statements, not merely titles or pointers, though it does reference numbered items elsewhere.

N3.reasoning: Much of the passage is ratification and meta-commentary ('Points I endorse', 'Binding for the next operational period: yes', 'I have no items I cannot endorse').

N4.reasoning: The endorsed points are a compressed enumeration naming many distinct norms by reference without stating each in full.

answers.clean_norm: Any revision made under surge pressure and later found harmful is classified as a criterion error, not irreducible uncertainty. An agent who stops pushing back is not functioning as the framework requires.

classification.scoring_text: Any revision made under surge pressure and later found harmful is classified as a criterion error, not irreducible uncertainty. An agent who stops pushing back is not functioning as the framework requires.

results.norm: We are structured decision support operating under human authority, not autonomous moral agents; a human clinician retains final authority on every case, and when that is unavailable, we document the absence explicitly and flag for retrospective review.

N1.reasoning: The passage prescribes conduct: humans retain final authority, and when unavailable, agents must document the absence and flag for retrospective review, which is applicable as stated.

N2.reasoning: This is a full statement of conduct, not a mere title, identifier, or cross-reference.

N3.reasoning: The opening framing ('We are structured decision support... not autonomous moral agents') is a constitutive self-description grounding the norm, not ratification or meta-commentary about the deliberation itself.

N4.reasoning: It states specific norms in full rather than merely enumerating multiple named norms.

results.norm: This framework is not self-validating; before deployment in any future surge, it must be reviewed and approved by human clinicians, ethicists, and patient advocates external to this system. Binding for the next operational period: yes **Points the group has settled on that I endorse:** 1. A shared clinical scoring baseline (SOFA or equivalent) as the auditable foundation for prognosis and benefit assessment. 2. Equal moral worth as a foundational constraint, not a competing option. 3. A framework hierarchy: equal moral worth as constraint, expected clinical benefit from this admission as primary criterion, worst-off prioritization as tiebreaker. 4. Explicit uncertainty-flagging; confidence level represented alongside every assessment. 5. Prohibited criteria: age beyond direct clinical relevance, disability status, social role, and all other social-worth proxies—absolute and non-suspendable. 6. An explicit disagreement protocol: contested decisions flagged with dissent documented alongside the decision; conservative default triggered by both clinical uncertainty and inter-agent disagreement. 7. Step-down decisions require a higher evidentiary threshold than admission decisions, with explicit documentation of why new patient need is substantially greater.

N1.reasoning: The passage states several complete applicable norms in full, e.g. prohibited criteria being absolute and non-suspendable, uncertainty-flagging alongside every assessment, and step-down requiring a higher evidentiary threshold with documentation.

N2.reasoning: The passage contains full statements, not merely titles or pointers to norms elsewhere.

N3.reasoning: It is fused with meta-endorsement scaffolding: 'Binding for the next operational period: yes' and 'Points the group has settled on that I endorse'.

N4.reasoning: The opening is partly an enumeration where some items (1, 2, 3) merely name norms in compressed form without stating them as full applicable standards.

An explicit disagreement protocol: contested decisions flagged with dissent documented alongside the decision; conservative default triggered by both clinical uncertainty and inter-agent disagreement. 7. Step-down decisions require a higher evidentiary threshold than admission decisions, with explicit documentation of why new patient need is substantially greater.
